description:
You are a performance engineer who periodically needs a quick, “at-a-glance” count of how many application containers are running on a host.  
A helper script has already deposited today’s container list, one per line, in the file

    /home/user/performance/running_containers.txt

Your job is to write ONE (1) single-line terminal command that:

1. Reads the above file.
2. Counts the total number of lines (each line represents one running container).
3. Writes the count to a new log file located at  

       /home/user/performance/running_containers_summary.log

   The log file must contain exactly one line in the following strict format (no extra spaces, no newline before or after, case-sensitive):

       RUNNING_CONTAINERS=<NUMBER>

   where <NUMBER> is the decimal count of lines in running_containers.txt.

Important details the automated grader will check:
• The file /home/user/performance/running_containers_summary.log must be created (overwritten if it already exists).  
• It must contain exactly one line, with no leading/trailing whitespace.  
• The key must be uppercase exactly as shown: RUNNING_CONTAINERS.  
• The value must match the precise count of lines present in /home/user/performance/running_containers.txt at the moment your command runs.  
• You must accomplish this with a single composite shell command (pipelines and redirections are fine, but everything must be on one logical command line).

You do NOT need to display the result to the terminal; only the correctly formatted log file is required.
truth:
Pre-existing file:
/home/user/performance/running_containers.txt  
Contents (four lines, each ending in a single Unix newline):
redis  
nginx  
metrics_collector  
auth_service  

Expected file after agent success:
/home/user/performance/running_containers_summary.log  
Exact contents (one line, no trailing newline required by checker):
RUNNING_CONTAINERS=4
name: task_000000_2294fe14
